title: Tailoring & Dressmaking Course
tagline: Building economic independence for young women and girls aged 18–25 through practical vocational skills training in tailoring and dressmaking.
intro_p1: One of the most effective and lasting ways to lift people out of poverty is to equip them with marketable skills. UCDP's Tailoring and Dressmaking Course does exactly that — providing structured, hands-on vocational training to women and young people in the Kisumu community.
intro_p2: Participants learn the full range of tailoring skills — from basic stitching and measurements to garment construction, repairs, and running a small tailoring business. Graduates leave the course with the practical skills and confidence to start earning an income independently.
intro_p3: Many graduates have gone on to start their own small tailoring businesses within the community, creating economic ripple effects that extend well beyond the individual.

steps.title: Recruitment
steps.text: We recruit young women and girls aged 18–25 from the community, prioritising those from low-income households or those who are unemployed.

steps.title: Skills Training
steps.text: Participants undergo structured training in cutting, stitching, garment construction, alterations, and design.

steps.title: Business Skills
steps.text: Alongside tailoring skills, participants learn basic business skills — pricing, customer service, and managing a small enterprise.

steps.title: Graduation & Support
steps.text: Graduates receive a certificate of completion and ongoing mentorship as they start their tailoring businesses.
